chapter: The d- and f- Block Elements

tests.test_name: the dblock and fblock elements Test-01

questions.question_text: The f – block elements are known as
questions.question_html: The f – block elements are known as
questions.explanation: The elements in which the valence electron enters in anti penultimate energy level i.e., (n - 2) f are known as f - block elements This name is because of the position of the valence electron is in the f - orbital. Since the last electron enters into (n - 2)f - orbital which is inner to the penultimate shell (For d-block elements), they are also called inner transition elements.

questions.question_text: 4d transition elements series are from
questions.question_html: 4d transition elements series are from
questions.explanation: 4d series arises from Y (4d 1 5s 2 ) to Cd (4d 10 5s 2 ).

questions.question_text: The inner transition metals of 5f series are known as
questions.question_html: The inner transition metals of 5f series are known as
questions.explanation: Inner transition metals of 5f series are known as Actinoids.

questions.question_text: Which among the following is the coinage metal?
questions.question_html: Which among the following is the coinage metal?
questions.explanation: Cu, Ag, Au are known as coinage metals. The elements of Group 11 are being called the coinage metals.

questions.question_text: Which among the following elements does not belong to the first transition series?
questions.question_html: Which among the following elements does not belong to the first transition series?
questions.explanation: Silver(Ag) belongs to the second transition series.

questions.question_text: Which among the following is not considered as a part of transition elements?
questions.question_html: Which among the following is not considered as a part of transition elements?
questions.explanation: The electronic configurations of Zn,Cd and Hg are represented by the general formula (n-1)d 10 ns 2 . The orbitals in these elements are completely filled in the ground state as well as in their common oxidation states. Therefore, they are not regarded as transition elements.

questions.question_text: Which among the following transition metal has the lowest melting point?
questions.question_html: Which among the following transition metal has the lowest melting point?
questions.explanation: Zn has the lowest melting point because it does not have any unpaired electrons (3d 10 4s 2 ) which are necessary to form an interatomic metallic bonding.

questions.question_text: Transition metals with highest melting point is
questions.question_html: Transition metals with highest melting point is
questions.explanation: The high melting points of these metals are attributed to the involvement of a greater number of electrons from (n-1)d in addition to the ns electrons in the interatomic metallic bonding. W belongs to 5d series and it has lot of unpaired electrons (5d 4 6s 2 ).

questions.question_text: Transition metals with lowest melting point is:
questions.question_html: Transition metals with lowest melting point is:
questions.explanation: Hg has the lowest melting point because it doesn't have any unpaired electrons and also it belongs to 5d series (5d 10 6s 2 ).

questions.question_text: The most stable oxidation state of Iron is:
questions.question_html: The most stable oxidation state of Iron is:
questions.explanation: Fe has d 5 configuration in +3 oxidation state.

questions.question_text: Electronic configuration of Cu 3+ is
questions.question_html: Electronic configuration of Cu3+ is
questions.explanation: Cu has an atomic number of 29 with an electronic configuration of [Ar]3d 10 4s 1 . On removal of 3 electrons to get Cu 3+ , electronic configuration changes to [Ar] 3d 8 .

questions.question_text: Electronic configuration of Mn 2+ is
questions.question_html: Electronic configuration of Mn2+ is
questions.explanation: Atomic number of Mn is 25 with electronic configuration of [Ar] 3d 5 4s 2 . On removal of two electrons to get Mn 2+ , the electronic configuration becomes [Ar] 3d 5 .

questions.question_text: Maximum magnetic moment is shown by
questions.question_html: Maximum magnetic moment is shown by
questions.explanation: The magnetic moment is directly proportional to the total number of unpaired electrons. So it will be maximum for the element having maximum unpaired electrons i.e. d 5 system.

questions.question_text: Maximum oxidation state is shown by
questions.question_html: Maximum oxidation state is shown by
questions.explanation: Os shows a maximum oxidation state of +8.

questions.question_text: Zinc carbonate is precipitated from zinc sulphate solution by the addition of
questions.question_html: Zinc carbonate is precipitated from zinc sulphate solution by the addition of
questions.explanation: ZnSO 4 + 2NaHCO 3 {tex}\to{/tex} ZnCO 3 + Na 2 SO 4 + H 2 O + CO 2

tests.test_name: the dblock and fblock elements Test-02

questions.question_text: Which of the following sublimes on heating?
questions.question_html: Which of the following sublimes on heating?
questions.explanation: HgCl 2 sublimes on heating and condenses in the form of small rhombic crystals. It was once used for the treatment of syphilis but is no longer used due to mercury toxicity.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following compound is deliquescent?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following compound is deliquescent?
questions.explanation: A deliquescent substance is a substance that absorbs moisture from the atmosphere until it dissolves in the absorbed moisture and forms a solution. ZnCl 2 is deliquescent.

questions.question_text: Which of the following ion has smallest radii?
questions.question_html: Which of the following ion has smallest radii?
questions.explanation: In period, when we move from left to right, atomic radii first decreases till nickel and then starts increasing. Hence Ni 2+ has the smallest radii.

questions.question_text: Which of the following pair of ions have the same magnetic moment?
questions.question_html: Which of the following pair of ions have the same magnetic moment?
questions.explanation: Cu 2+ ,Ti 3+ have the same number of unpaired electrons i.e.1 and hence have the same magnetic moment.

questions.question_text: Which of the following metals have only one oxidation state?
questions.question_html: Which of the following metals have only one oxidation state?
questions.explanation: Al is not a d-block element but p- block element. It shows only one oxidation state. While rest all others are d-block elements which show variable oxidation states.

questions.question_text: Mohr’s salt is a:
questions.question_html: Mohr’s salt is a:
questions.explanation: Mohr Salt is FeSO 4 .(NH 4 ) 2 .6H 2 O. It is obtained by a combination of two different salts which were crystallized in the same regular ionic lattice. It is formed by an equimolar mixture of ferrous sulphate and ammonium sulphate. Since two salts are involved, Mohr salt is a double salt.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is an example of non – typical transition elements?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is an example of non – typical transition elements?
questions.explanation: Zn, Cd, and Hg are not considered as transition elements because they do not have incompletely filled d orbital in its ground state or stable oxidation state.

questions.question_text: Zr and Hf have almost equal atomic and ionic radii because:
questions.question_html: Zr and Hf have almost equal atomic and ionic radii because:

options.text: both belong to same transition series
options.html: both belong to same transition series

questions.explanation: As after lanthanum, electron filling takes place in f-subshell. Electrons present in f-subshell didn't do good shielding due to which with the increasing atomic number or increasing effective nuclear charge size gets constricted and the size of Hf and Zr becomes almost equal.

questions.question_text: What is the shape of Fe(CN) 5 molecule?
questions.question_html: What is the shape of Fe(CN)5 molecule?
questions.explanation: Fe has 5 carbonyl ligands around it i.e. CN=5. It has trigonal bipyramidal geometry with dsp 3 hybridization.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compound is amphoteric?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compound is amphoteric?
questions.explanation: Amphoteric substances are those which can act as either acids or bases. Cr(OH) 3 in water produces either salts (chromates) if combined with alkaline hydroxides (strong bases) such as potassium or sodium chromates and it can produce chromium salts if combined with strong acids (HCl, HF, H 2 SO 4, etc.) giving chromium sulfate, chloride, fluoride, etc.

questions.question_text: Which of the following sulphides is soluble in aqua regia?
questions.question_html: Which of the following sulphides is soluble in aqua regia?
questions.explanation: HgS is only soluble in aqua regia. Aqua regia is a mixture of concentrated HCl and concentrated HNO 3 in ratio 3:1. The solubilization turns Hg in HgCl 4 2- , a complex called chloromercurate which is stable in water. 3HgS + 2NO 3 - + 12Cl - + 8H 3 O + {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} 3HgCl 4 2- + 2NO + 12H 2 O + 3S

questions.question_text: Silver is refined by the cupellation process. The process removes the impurity of:
questions.question_html: Silver is refined by the cupellation process. The process removes the impurity of:
questions.explanation: Cupellation is a refining process in metallurgy, where ores or alloyed metals are treated under very high temperatures and have controlled operations to separate noble metals, like gold and silver, from base metals like lead, copper, zinc or bismuth, present in the ore. Cupellation removes the impurity of Pb from silver. The reaction is given below: Ag(s) + 2Pb(s) + O 2 (g) {tex}\to{/tex} 2PbO(absorbed) + Ag(l)

questions.question_text: Mohr’s salt is a better volumetric agent then ferrous sulphate because:
questions.question_html: Mohr’s salt is a better volumetric agent then ferrous sulphate because:

options.text: It is less readily oxidized in the solid state
options.html: It is less readily oxidized in the solid state

options.text: All the chemical reactions given by ferrous sulphate are given by Mohr’s salt
options.html: All the chemical reactions given by ferrous sulphate are given by Mohr’s salt

options.text: It does not effloresce like ferrous sulphate
options.html: It does not effloresce like ferrous sulphate

questions.correct_option_text: It is less readily oxidized in the solid state
questions.explanation: Mohr’s salt is a better volumetric agent than ferrous sulphate because it is less readily oxidized in the solid state. Also, Mohr’s salt is more stable than FeSO 4 .

questions.question_text: Red hot steel rods on suddenly immersing in water become:
questions.question_html: Red hot steel rods on suddenly immersing in water become:
questions.explanation: Red hot steel rods on suddenly immersing in water become hard and brittle.

questions.question_text: Which of the following methods is based on distribution law?
questions.question_html: Which of the following methods is based on distribution law?
questions.explanation: The Parkes process is for removing silver from lead. It is an example of liquid-liquid extraction.

tests.test_name: the dblock and fblock elements Test-03

questions.question_text: Which of the following methods is based on distribution law?
questions.question_html: Which of the following methods is based on distribution law?
questions.explanation: The Parkes process is for removing silver from lead. It is an example of liquid-liquid extraction.

questions.question_text: How many carats are in 87.5% gold?
questions.question_html: How many carats are in 87.5% gold?
questions.explanation: M g = 87.5 and M m = 100 Carat = {tex}24{\text{ }} \times {\text{ }}\left( {\frac{{{M_g}}}{{{M_m}}}} \right) = 24 \times \frac{{87.5}}{{100}} = 21{/tex}

questions.question_text: How many d–block elements have the ability to evolve hydrogen gas from 2% nitric acid?
questions.question_html: How many d–block elements have the ability to evolve hydrogen gas from 2% nitric acid?
questions.explanation: Both Zn and Fe can displace Hydrogen from HNO 3 .

questions.question_text: Haemoglobin and chlorophyll contain:
questions.question_html: Haemoglobin and chlorophyll contain:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin contains Fe and Chlorophyll contains Mg.

questions.question_text: Which chromium compound is widely used in tanning of leather?
questions.question_html: Which chromium compound is widely used in tanning of leather?
questions.explanation: K 2 SO 4 .Cr 2 (SO 4 ) 3 .24H 2 O is used in tanning of leather.

questions.question_text: The product of oxidation of I – with MnO 4 - in acidic medium is:
questions.question_html: The product of oxidation of I– with MnO4- in acidic medium is:
questions.explanation: Iodine is liberated from potassium iodide. 10I - + 2MnO 4 - + 16H + {tex} \rightarrow{/tex} 2Mn 2+ + 8H 2 O + 5I 2

questions.question_text: In dilute alkaline solution, MnO 4 - changes to:
questions.question_html: In dilute alkaline solution, MnO4- changes to:
questions.explanation: In alkaline medium, the reduction of MnO 4 - take place to form MnO 2. The chemical equation for this change is given below as: MnO 4 - (aq) + 2H 2 O(l) + 3e - → MnO 2 (s)+ 4OH - (aq)

questions.question_text: Among the following, which bivalent ion of the first transition series shows a maximum magnetic moment?
questions.question_html: Among the following, which bivalent ion of the first transition series shows a maximum magnetic moment?
questions.explanation: Mn 2+ has d 5 configuration so a maximum number of unpaired electrons and hence maximum magnetic moment. This magnetic moment can be calculated by using the spin only formula: {tex}{\mu _{so}} = \angle n(n + 2){/tex} , where n= number of unpaired electrons.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is called chromic acid?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is called chromic acid?
questions.explanation: H 2 CrO 4 is chromic acid. It is actually formed by mixing concentrated sulphuric acid to a dichromate like sodium dichromate which may contain a variety of compounds, including solid chromium trioxide.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following combines with Fe 2+ ion to form a brown complex?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following combines with Fe2+ ion to form a brown complex?
questions.explanation: The brown ring test is usually carried out by adding dilute ferrous sulphate solution to an aqueous solution containing nitrate ion and then carefully adding concentrated sulphuric acid along the sides of the test tube. A brown ring at the interface between the solution and sulphuric acid layers indicates the presence of nitrate ion in solution. {tex}{\left[ {Fe{{\left( {{H_2}O} \right)}_5}NO} \right]^{2 + }}{/tex} is a brown colour complex formed. {tex}NO_3+3Fe^{2+}+4H^+\to NO+Fe^{3+}+2H_2O{/tex} {tex}[Fe(H_2O)]^{2+}+NO\to [Fe(H_2O)_5(NO)]^{2+}+H_2O{/tex}

questions.question_text: The Lucas reagent is:
questions.question_html: The Lucas reagent is:
questions.explanation: Lucas test is done to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols. Lucas reagent is ZnCl 2 + HCl.

questions.question_text: Vitamin B 12 contains:
questions.question_html: Vitamin B12 contains:
questions.explanation: Chemically vit. B 12 is C 63 H 88 O 14 N 14 PCo .It contains cobalt in +3 oxidation state.

questions.question_text: Which is least soluble in water?
questions.question_html: Which is least soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Ag 2 S is least soluble in water. It's K sp (solubility product) in water is 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 -51 . K sp value indicates how soluble a compound is.

questions.question_text: A reduction in the atomic size with an increase in atomic number is characteristic of the elements of:
questions.question_html: A reduction in the atomic size with an increase in atomic number is characteristic of the elements of:
questions.explanation: In f-block elements with an increase in atomic number, atomic radii decrease smoothly due to lanthanide contraction.

questions.question_text: Nitrous oxide is evolved when Zn is treated with:
questions.question_html: Nitrous oxide is evolved when Zn is treated with:
questions.explanation: 4Zn(s) + 10HNO 3 (l) {tex}\to{/tex} 4Zn(NO 3 ) 2 (s) + N 2 O(g) + 5H 2 O(l)

tests.test_name: the dblock and fblock elements Test-04

questions.question_text: Ferrous sulphate on heating gives:
questions.question_html: Ferrous sulphate on heating gives:
questions.explanation: FeSO 4 on heating decomposes to SO 2 and SO 3 along with solid Fe 2 O 3 as given below: 2FeSO 4 (s) {tex} \to {/tex} Fe 2 O 3 (s) + SO 2 (g)+ SO 3 (g)

questions.question_text: In the reaction, SnCl 2 + HgCl 2 {tex}\to{/tex} A + SnCl 4 , A is:
questions.question_html: In the reaction, SnCl2 + HgCl2 {tex}\to{/tex} A + SnCl4, A is:
questions.explanation: Tin(II) chloride react with mercury (II) chloride in acidic medium to produce mercury and tin (IV) chloride as given below: SnCl 2 + HgCl 2 {tex}\to{/tex} Hg + SnCl 4

questions.question_text: Ag + ion is isoelectronic with:
questions.question_html: Ag+ ion is isoelectronic with:
questions.explanation: The atomic number of Ag is 47 with an electronic configuration of [Kr] 4d 10 5s 1 whereas for Cd, the atomic number is 48 and electronic configuration is [Kr] 4d 10 5s 2 . Ag + is isoelectronic with Cd 2+ , since the electronic configuration of both of them is [Kr] 4d 10 .

questions.question_text: Ni 2+ in traces can be tested using
questions.question_html: Ni2+ in traces can be tested using
questions.explanation: Ni 2+ forms complex with DMG which is red in colour.

questions.question_text: The compound which is widely used as a white pigment is:
questions.question_html: The compound which is widely used as a white pigment is:
questions.explanation: ZnO is white in color. This is because Zn is in +2 oxidation state with d 10 system having no unpaired electrons and hence the absence of d-d transition.

questions.question_text: The catalyst used in Deacon’s process is:
questions.question_html: The catalyst used in Deacon’s process is:
questions.explanation: CuCl 2 is used as a catalyst in Deacon’s Process.

questions.question_text: Lanthanoid contraction is:
questions.question_html: Lanthanoid contraction is:

options.text: the filling of 4f before 5d orbital resulting in a regular decrease in atomic radii.
options.html: the filling of 4f before 5d orbital resulting in a regular decrease in atomic radii.

options.text: decrease in stability of higher oxidation states of lanthanides.
options.html: decrease in stability of higher oxidation states of lanthanides.

options.text: the decrease in the ionic character of lanthanides with an increase in the oxidation state.
options.html: the decrease in the ionic character of lanthanides with an increase in the oxidation state.

options.text: ions of the same charge in a given series showing a progressive decrease in radius with increasing atomic number.
options.html: ions of the same charge in a given series showing a progressive decrease in radius with increasing atomic number.

questions.correct_option_text: the filling of 4f before 5d orbital resulting in a regular decrease in atomic radii.
questions.explanation: The filling of 4f before 5d orbital results in a regular decrease in atomic radii called Lanthanoid contraction which essentially compensates for the expected increase in atomic size with increasing atomic number. The net result of the lanthanoid contraction is that the second and the third d series exhibit similar radii and have very similar physical and chemical properties much more than that expected on the basis of usual family relationships.

questions.question_text: Annealing process involves:
questions.question_html: Annealing process involves:

options.text: Heating the steel bright red and then cooling slowly.
options.html: Heating the steel bright red and then cooling slowly.

options.text: Heating the rods of iron embedded in charcoal.
options.html: Heating the rods of iron embedded in charcoal.

options.text: Heating the steel upto moderate temperature and cooling very fastly.
options.html: Heating the steel upto moderate temperature and cooling very fastly.

options.text: Heating the rods of iron in ammonia.
options.html: Heating the rods of iron in ammonia.

questions.correct_option_text: Heating the steel bright red and then cooling slowly.
questions.explanation: Annealing is a heat treatment that alters the physical and sometimes chemical properties of a material to increase it's ductility and reduce it's hardness, making it more workable. It involves heating a material above it's recrystallisation temperature, maintaining a suitable temperature for a suitable amount of time, and then cooling.

questions.question_text: How many electrons are involved in the reduction of KMnO 4 in the basic medium?
questions.question_html: How many electrons are involved in the reduction of KMnO4 in the basic medium?
questions.explanation: In the basic medium, the following reduction reaction takes place for permanganate ion. {tex}MnO_4^-+e^-\to MnO_4^{2-}{/tex} Thus 1 e - is involved.

questions.question_text: Calomel is the name of:
questions.question_html: Calomel is the name of:
questions.explanation: Mercurous chloride is known as calomel (Hg 2 Cl 2 ).

questions.question_text: Silver ornaments turn black by the presence of which gas in the atmosphere?
questions.question_html: Silver ornaments turn black by the presence of which gas in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Silver ornaments turns black coming in contact with H 2 S due to formation of Ag 2 S. The chemical equation for this change can be represented as given below: 2Ag(s) + H 2 S(g) → Ag 2 S(s) + H 2 (g)

questions.question_text: In the reaction, {tex}{\text{HgC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}} + {\text{ 4KI}}\;\xrightarrow{{}}{\text{A }} + {\text{ 2KCl}}{/tex} A is:
questions.question_html: In the reaction, {tex}{\text{HgC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}} + {\text{ 4KI}}\;\xrightarrow{{}}{\text{A }} + {\text{ 2KCl}}{/tex}  A is:
questions.explanation: HgCl 2 + 4KI {tex}\to{/tex} K 2 [HgI 4 ] + 2KCl

questions.question_text: Electronic configuration of Fe 3+ is:
questions.question_html: Electronic configuration of Fe3+ is:
questions.explanation: Atomic number of Fe is 26 and it's electronic configuration is [Ar] 3d 6 4s 2 . When we remove 3 electrons, it becomes [Ar] 3d 5 .

questions.question_text: The yellow colour of the chromate changes to orange on acidification due to the formation of:
questions.question_html: The yellow colour of the chromate changes to orange on acidification due to the formation of:
questions.explanation: Chromate ion (CrO 4 2- ) changes to dichromate ion (Cr 2 O 7 2- ) on acidification. 2 CrO 4 2- + 2 H + → Cr 2 O 7 2- + H 2 O

questions.question_text: Identify Prussian blue from the following:
questions.question_html: Identify Prussian blue from the following:
questions.explanation: Fe 4 [Fe(CN) 6 ] 3 is Prussian Blue. The intense blue colour of Prussian blue is associated with the energy of the transfer of electrons from Fe(II) to Fe(III). Many such mixed-valence compounds absorb certain wavelengths of visible light resulting from intervalence charge transfer.
